mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, labor market tightening reduces recession probability and boosts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days in backtests, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates expanding capacity utilization and stronger corporate output, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns during post-shock recovery periods show no positive correlation with industrial production growth, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising household income, which increases consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant after adjusting for seasonal employment fluctuations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction after controlling for oil price shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth outpaces retail sales growth indicates weak household consumption relative to overall output, suppressing corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when government spending is excluded from GDP calculations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, reducing corporate profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal loses predictive power when commodity price shocks drive the production drop.
